/cwe/weakness/1069.Name: Empty Exception Block
/cwe/weakness/1069.Description: An invokable code block contains an exception handling block that does not contain any code, i.e. is empty.
/cwe/weakness/1069.ExtendedDescription: When an exception handling block (such as a Catch and Finally block) is used, but that block is empty, this can prevent the product from running reliably. If the relevant code is reachable by an attacker, then this reliability problem might introduce a vulnerability.

Entries.IntroText: In the following Java example, the code catches an ArithmeticException.

Entries.BodyText: Since the exception block is empty, no action is taken.
In the code below the exception has been logged and the bad execution has been handled in the desired way allowing the program to continue in an expected way.

PotentialMitigations.Description: For every exception block add code that handles the specific exception in the way intended by the application.
